title: Impact Events and the People Who Drive Them
display_title: Impact Events and the People Who Drive Them
wikitext:
[]: Impact Events and the People Who Drive Them             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jul 14, 2013 11:38 am Impact Events: What They Are, Why They Occur, and Who Benefits From Them This is not true. Kind of an odd thing to say, given the topic, but it needs to be addressed. Evangelion is a puzzle box, and deliberately so; it is flawed in some respects and incomplete in others, and on top of that we, the viewers, engage with it using imperfect minds, with flawed understanding of the basic material. Most of us are not the intended audience for the work, and we do not understand the culture in which it was produced. That means it would be the height of arrogance to label any given interpretation "Truth". In lieu of that, all we can do is study the puzzle before us and assemble its pieces as best we can, taking comfort in the resulting image even if it is a distorted parody of the author's original intent. This, then, is how the puzzle's pieces fit together for me. So, what am I doing here? Simply put, the goal is to identify and analyze the mechanics of Impact in Evangelion, and from there determine what happened during each Impact event, why it occurred, and what it means for the major players in the show. In other words I will be looking at the major events of each Impact, examining the goals of the FAR, identifying Seele's agenda and methods, and finally discussing Yui's actions and goals up through the end of Third Impact. Some caveats before I start: For the purposes of this narrative there is no magic. Some of the major events might be dressed up as incantations, or prophecies, or supernatural occurrences, but all of that is just window dressing in my view. Everything, start to finish, operates as products of reason and technology. Some may disagree with this assessment, and that's fine; that just means this approach is not for you. But realize that this is an axiom governing this piece and critique accordingly. This is, at least in principle, a work of interpretation, not speculation. While the show does make limited use of such things as pocket dimensions (the Chambers of Guf, Leliel's body), PWM, and quantum strangeness, I will not be invoking these to explain what's going on with the Seeds and Impact events, both because it's unnecessary and because I feel doing so is a copout. I likewise will not be inventing or relying on extra-canonical scenes from the show, since going that route amounts to rampant speculation at best and second-rate fanfiction at worst. There are some minor exceptions here and there (e.g. since Seele wanted Lilith to do their bidding they presumably asked her first before coming up with torturous plots to bend her to their will), but I'll be using them only if the show itself directly implies their existence. Everything in the show happens for a reason. The conclusions "it just doesn't make sense" or "it's just there because it looks cool" or "Anno was just making shit up" do not fly. Mind you, that doesn't mean they aren't true ; it's entirely possible (nay, almost certain) that all of the above come into play at various points in the show, but for the purposes of explaining what's going on in the setting and why they are simply not acceptable explanations. Purely visual effects won't be addressed (no one really cares why the cross explosions look the way they do), but if it affects the plot in some way you'll get the best explanation I can muster. My experience with the scenarios in the Neon Genesis Evangelion 2 video game is limited, and I'm sure my theories here might have to be modified to accommodate them when/if more are brought to light. If complete translations of said endings ever become available (preferably done by native speakers, since many plot points in NGE seem to turn heavily on subtle shades of meaning that would only be understood by native speakers) I'll revise accordingly. I won't be making use of script quotes or screencaps here. This is quite deliberate: I don't want you to look at scenes, phrases, and the like in isolation. Rather, I want you to consider the show it its entirety when looking at how things work. When I reference a scene, look at the whole of that scene, from the visuals to the dialogue, and process it as a gestalt. See if you get the same vibe I do from the events therein, and if you come to a different conclusion (with the above caveats in mind) ask why we're processing the scene differently. It is my hope that this approach will head off many minor disagreements before they start, leaving us only with fundamental differences in approach to deal with. Editorial Note  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Jul 14, 2013 12:17 pm Okay, I'll bite. Everything makes a certain amount of sense, enough for me to be able to discuss it within the contents of this framework. By saying, "Yui wanted to save the Angels," do you mean that she desired to re-make earth into a place for Angels, and build a human world somewhere else? In which case, I believe her final words, "Will you be alright?" take on a very different meaning. Assuming that you mean Yui intends to make the earth a world for Angels, she's asking him if he's going to be okay living in this new world inhabited by man and Angel alike. If Shinji knows this, his strangling Asuka takes on a new meaning as well. Perhaps he doesn't want Asuka to have to live in this world. However, he seems to blissfully naive on this point; his last words to Yui seem to indicate that all he is worried about is living alongside other humans. Just some fanwankery here, and maybe you meant something totally different about preserving the Angels and all of this is merely so much bullshit.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jul 14, 2013 12:20 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Okay, I'll bite. Everything makes a certain amount of sense, enough for me to be able to discuss it within the contents of this framework. By saying, "Yui wanted to save the Angels," do you mean that she desired to re-make earth into a place for Angels, and build a human world somewhere else? In which case, I believe her final words, "Will you be alright?" take on a very different meaning.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Jul 14, 2013 12:24 pm What did you find to demonstrate that she had anything with her to continue on the line of Adams? She lacks Adam himself (still inside dying GNR) or anything from Adam's lineage to do the work for her. It doesn't seem like there's any way that Yui could hope to be any sort of a progenitor for the race of the Angels.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jul 14, 2013 12:33 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: What did you find to demonstrate that she had anything with her to continue on the line of Adams? She lacks Adam himself (still inside dying GNR) or anything from Adam's lineage to do the work for her. It doesn't seem like there's any way that Yui could hope to be any sort of a progenitor for the race of the Angels.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sun Jul 14, 2013 12:36 pm Yui approves this essay. And so do I.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Jul 14, 2013 12:43 pm Is there anything that states that more souls can't be created, and that all souls indiscriminately come from the COG? (I actually like this acronym for the Chamber of Guf. A lot. Let's see if I can get it introduced.) I'm not religious, so I don't know how rebirth is actually supposed to work pertaining to souls and whatnot. Is there only one pool of them, and they just get circulated through every human that is born? Or do we create new souls? If the latter is the case, perhaps there are only 14 angels because they were meant to reproduce in some manner.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Jul 14, 2013 1:54 pm So...what are the MP Evas trying to accomplish, exactly?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jul 14, 2013 2:02 pm AuraTwilight wrote: So...what are the MP Evas trying to accomplish, exactly?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Jul 14, 2013 5:01 pm I just took the time to read the entirety of this essay, and this was my reaction before even reaching the end: Fan. Tas. Tic. Finally, a unified theory has been presented that ties together the meaning of the Impacts with the mechanics which not only comports with all the evidence from the show and NGE2, but is also compatible with a wide variety of theories about the specifics of the mechanics. It manages to encompass both my own and Bagheera's conclusions about such details as who and what have what Fruits -- how is that even possible?! There is nothing, except niggling unimportant details like whether Seele tried to persuade Lilith , that I can find disagreement with in this essay. The broad sweep of it rings resoundingly true to me, and has finally provided for me a meaning to what was once so much indecipherable noise. Bravo, Bagheera. And welcome back. Disclaimer: Reactions always subject to change with time. Void where prohibited.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Jul 14, 2013 5:46 pm Wow, that was an awesome way to make your return to EGF, Bags! I especially liked the "passing on the torch" bit.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Jul 14, 2013 6:03 pm riffraff11235 wrote: Wow, that was an awesome way to make your return to EGF, Bags! I especially liked the "passing on the torch" bit.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Sun Jul 14, 2013 6:39 pm riffraff11235 wrote: Wow, that was an awesome way to make your return to EGF, Bags! I especially liked the "passing on the torch" bit.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Jul 14, 2013 6:53 pm What I like about it perhaps most is that it's far from a definitive answer, and admits it up front: It doesn't present itself as The End All Be All Answer to Everything like so many megaposts do. It is, merely, an interpretation, and it is more persuasive in its humility, but at the same time I am able to accept it as no more than a possibility, non-threatening to all analysis that has come before and will come after, and that lack of risk of it being taken as dogma makes it only the more fun to entertain and examine. It doesn't pick any fights.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sun Jul 14, 2013 7:31 pm riffraff11235 wrote: I especially liked the "passing on the torch" bit.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Jul 14, 2013 7:45 pm One of the less important details that I'd like to question is, what is the evidence that Lilith was discovered before Adam? Lilith is not called the First Angel, Adam is, and I always assumed from certain subtle implications in the CI that Adam was discovered first. That also suffices to explain why all the experiments, etc, were done with Adam first rather than Lilith. I'm hoping that the assumption that Seele asked Lilith before resorting to Adam was simply based on a previously unquestioned assumption that Lilith must have been discovered before Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Jul 14, 2013 7:47 pm I've assumed that Seele were the ones naming the Angels, and presumably Adam would have been identified as the First Angel in the Secret Dead Sea Scrolls.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jul 14, 2013 8:04 pm Monk Ed wrote: One of the less important details that I'd like to question is, what is the evidence that Lilith was discovered before Adam? Lilith is not called the First Angel, Adam is, and I always assumed from certain subtle implications in the CI that Adam was discovered first. That also suffices to explain why all the experiments, etc, were done with Adam first rather than Lilith. I'm hoping that the assumption that Seele asked Lilith before resorting to Adam was simply based on a previously unquestioned assumption that Lilith must have been discovered before Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Jul 14, 2013 8:15 pm Bagheera wrote: Regardless of which was discovered first Adam would be the first "Angel" since he arrived on the world before Lilith did. He is thus necessarily first, and she second. From there the Angels are numbered according to when they are predicted to mature.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sun Jul 14, 2013 9:20 pm Great post bags! I'm particularly impressed with the structure of your entire post, specifically how you got the basic mechanics out of the way and left the more speculative things for later. It served to make the whole read much more fluid and enjoyable. I like your interpretation of Yui's character and her actions, it's seems like she was way ahead of the game. However it really makes me wonder about her father and exactly how active a role he had in setting the groundwork along with Yui, the idea of them being cohorts is a fascinating thought.
